to fasten upon; hence, to seize, be strong (figuratively, courageous, causatively strengthen, cure, help, repair, fortify), obstinate; to bind, restrain, conquer
Jehovah, Jewish national name of God

tokens.text: not unto them as the LORD
tokens.range: Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
the heart; also used (figuratively) very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything
Mosheh, the Israelite lawgiver
Paroh, a general title of Egyptian kings
to hear intelligently (often with implication of attention, obedience, etc.; causatively, to tell, etc.)

flags.note: יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.

tokens.range: a word; by implication, a matter (as spoken of) or thing; adverbially, a cause
Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
something bought, i.e. property, but only livestock; abstractly, acquisition
a servant
to loosen, i.e. relinquish, permit, etc.
a field (as flat)
to put (used in a great variety of applications, literal, figurative, inferentially, and elliptically)

flags.note: יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.

Mosheh, the Israelite lawgiver
to stand, in various relations (literal and figurative, intransitive and transitive)
